The user wants the layout of the provided document extracted into a JSON object with bounding boxes.

1.  **Identify the main sections:**
    *   Header (Logo, Title, Document Info)
    *   Section 6.5 Electrical Characteristics (Table)
    *   Section 6.6 Timing Requirements (Table)
    *   Footer (Copyright, Links, Page Number)

2.  **Process Header:**
    *   Logo: Top left.
    *   Title: "TPS562200, TPS563200" Top right.
    *   Document ID: "SLVSCB0E..." Top right.
    *   Website: "www.ti.com" Top left.

3.  **Process Section 6.5:**
    *   Title: "6.5 Electrical Characteristics"
    *   Condition: "TJ = -40°C to 150°C..."
    *   Table: This is a complex table. I need to break it down row by row, or at least capture the main structure.
        *   Headers: PARAMETER, TEST CONDITIONS, MIN, TYP, MAX, UNIT.
        *   Sub-headers: SUPPLY CURRENT, LOGIC THRESHOLD, VFB VOLTAGE..., MOSFET, CURRENT LIMIT, THERMAL SHUTDOWN, OUTPUT UNDERTVOLTAGE..., UVLO.
        *   Data rows: I need to capture the text content and approximate bounding boxes for each cell.

4.  **Process Section 6.6:**
    *   Title: "6.6 Timing Requirements"
    *   Table: Similar structure to the previous one.
        *   Headers: MIN, TYP, MAX, UNIT.
        *   Sub-headers: ON-TIME TIMER CONTROL, SOFT START.
        *   Data rows.

5.  **Process Footer:**
    *   Copyright text.
    *   Feedback link.
    *   Page number.
    *   Product Folder Links.

6.  **Refine Bounding Boxes:** I will estimate coordinates based on a standard 1000x1000 grid or similar relative scale, but since I need to output specific coordinates, I will use the visual position. *Self-correction: The prompt asks for bboxes. I will generate the JSON structure with the text and the corresponding [ymin, xmin, ymax, xmax] coordinates.*

    *   *Header:*
        *   Logo: [36, 90, 68, 246]
        *   Title: [55, 746, 68, 912]
